Why Brute-Force Vector Search Wins at Small Scale

When a corpus holds fewer than ten thousand embeddings, a linear scan with cosine similarity completes in single-digit milliseconds on commodity hardware. Approximate nearest neighbor indexes buy their speed with build time, memory overhead, and recall loss that small corpora never amortize.

We measured a 5,000 by 1,024 dimension scan at roughly three milliseconds in release mode. The index alternative added a dependency, an unsafe FFI boundary, and a background build step, and saved nothing measurable at this size.

The lesson generalizes: pick the simplest structure that meets the latency budget, and measure before adding infrastructure.
